2606.26968.title: RedVox: Safety and Fairness Gaps in Speech Models Across Languages
2606.26968.paper_summary: Speech-capable models are increasingly deployed in real-world applications across languages. Yet their safety and fairness beyond English settings and under naturalistic conditions remain understudied. We survey safety reporting practices across state-of-the-art speech model releases, finding that only 8% document any multilingual analysis. To address this gap, we introduce RedVox, a multilingual safety and fairness benchmark for audio and speech built on real voices, covering unsafe and unfair stereotypical requests across five languages (English, French, Italian, Spanish, and German). Evaluating eight state-of-the-art models, we find that vulnerabilities persist even under non-adversarial conditions, worsen in non-English languages, and are amplified when the request comes from a spoken input. Finally, by surveying the participants who contributed to RedVox, we document the unique personal and privacy challenges of collecting speech data with human participants, pointing to broader sociotechnical challenges in naturalistic speech safety research.
2606.26968.paper_summary_zh: 語音能力模型在各種語言的現實應用中越來越多地被部署。然而，它們在英語環境之外以及在自然條件下的安全性和公平性仍然未受到充分研究。我們調查了最先進的語音模型發布中的安全報告實踐，發現只有 8% 的文檔中提及任何多語言分析。為了解決這一差距，我們推出了 RedVox，一個基於真實聲音的多語言音頻和語音安全與公平基準，涵蓋了五種語言（英語、法語、意大利語、西班牙語和德語）中的不安全和不公平的刻板請求。在評估八個最先進的模型時，我們發現即使在非對抗性條件下，脆弱性仍然存在，在非英語語言中惡化，並且當請求來自口語輸入時會被放大。最後，通過調查參與 RedVox 的參與者，我們記錄了與人類參與者收集語音數據的獨特個人和隱私挑戰，指出了自然語音安全研究中的更廣泛社會技術挑戰。
2606.26968.author: Beatrice Savoldi et.al.
2606.26968.authors: Beatrice Savoldi, Sara Papi, Wafa Aissa, Matteo Negri, Luisa Bentivogli
